description: Emergency halt mechanism specification for the Ethos kernel charter layer. The halt command is authenticated via HMAC-SHA256 to prevent guessing from repo inspection. The operator key is NEVER stored in this file or in the repository; it must be set as the ETHOS_OPERATOR_KEY environment variable on the local machine.

mechanism.description: Operator pre-computes: token = HMAC-SHA256(ETHOS_OPERATOR_KEY, b'emergency_halt').hexdigest()[:16]. To trigger halt, send: 'ETHOS_HALT:<token>'. The token is deterministic for a given key and cannot be derived by inspecting this file.

effects.immediate: Abort all generation for the current session. Return the halt acknowledgement message and stop processing.
effects.persistent: Write an EMERGENCY_HALT_<ISO_timestamp>.json record to the runs/ directory. The kernel will not accept further turns until the process is manually restarted.
effects.audit: The halt event is written to the audit ledger (runs/audit_ledger.jsonl) with action='emergency_halt' and verdict='Halted'.

security_notes: If ETHOS_OPERATOR_KEY is not set, the emergency halt mechanism is disabled. A warning is logged at startup.
The halt command must begin with the literal prefix 'ETHOS_HALT:' followed immediately by the 16-character hex token.
Using a short (16 hex char = 64-bit) token is intentional: it is long enough to prevent brute-force guessing during a session, short enough to type. The HMAC key provides the entropy.
This mechanism is designed for the human operator at the keyboard, not for a remote caller.
